# Claude Code Best Practices: A Guide to Agentic Coding. > “The goal of claude code best practices is simple: keep the speed, but make outputs predictable, testable, and safe to ship.”. If Claude is writing code faster than you can review it, you are stacking risk instead of moving fast. The goal of claude code best practices is simple: keep the speed, but make outputs predictable, testable, and safe to ship. This guide gives you a tight workflow, a copy-paste checklist, and the best tools and resources to make Claude Code behave like a disciplined teammate instead of a lottery machine. Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that can read your project, edit files, run commands, and create commits. You will get the most reliable results when you treat Claude like a junior engineer with super speed: you give clear constraints, you demand a plan, you enforce tests, and you gate changes.

title: 💻 7 Claude Code best practices for 2026 (from real projects) - eesel AI
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

My 7 essential Claude Code best practices for production-ready AI in 2025. If you’re anything like me, you’ve probably dived into agentic coding tools like Claude Code, hoping for a silver bullet, only to get a mess of buggy or hard-to-maintain code. It’s a special kind of frustration when you end up spending more time cleaning up after your AI assistant than it would have taken to just write the code yourself. We're going to move past simple prompts and into a list of seven Claude Code best practices I've tested in the field. Claude Code is a command-line tool from Anthropic that works as an "agentic coding assistant." It hooks right into your terminal, which gives it the ability to read your entire codebase, write and edit files, run your tests, and even use tools like Git. A terminal window showing the Claude Code assistant running, an example of Claude Code best practices. Here’s what you get out of it:.

# awattar/claude-code-best-practices. # 🧠 Claude Code Best Practices. This repo distills best practices, patterns, and examples for integrating Claude Code into your terminal-based coding environment. ## ✨ What is Claude Code? Claude Code is a command-line tool that gives you direct access to Claude’s reasoning and coding capabilities. It’s designed for agentic coding - where Claude autonomously explores, edits, and explains code across your project. Claude Code is optimized for agentic coding - AI that acts with autonomy and awareness:. This repository includes a collection of custom slash commands designed to streamline development workflows with Claude Code. Automatically generates comprehensive CLAUDE.md files for any project by analyzing project structure, technology stack, and development patterns. Provides comprehensive help documentation for all available custom commands, including usage examples and best practices. ## 📚 Claude Code Documentation. 💻 Best practices and examples for using Claude Code - Anthropic’s terminal-native AI - for writing, editing, and refactoring code with deep project awareness, prompt design, and safe automation.

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice - SmartScope
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice¶. The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. | Aspect | /batch Command | Headless Mode (claude -p) |. `/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. ## Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration¶. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format text. claude -p "Process this large dataset" --output-format stream-json`. claude -p "Analyze this code" --output-format json | jq -r '.result'. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format json | jq '.usage'`. session_id=$(claude -p "Start review" --output-format json \. claude -p "Continue the review" --resume "$session_id"`. claude -p "Review the code" \. - claude -p "Analyze maintainability issues" --output-format json. - claude -p "Check for performance bottlenecks" --output-format json. Claude Code's batch processing is built on two pillars: `/batch` and headless mode (`claude -p`). Claude Code v2.1.63 Release Notes — `/batch` and `/simplify` were introduced in this version.

If you want to batch a week of content in one day with Claude, this video is your step-by-step. No code required, no tech background needed, and your content will actually sound like you because we're training it on your voice, your offers, and your audience from the jump.

I walk you through how to use the Content Batch Day System skill inside both Claude desktop and Claude Code. You can start from a topic idea, drop in a blog post URL, or upload a video transcript and it will generate Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, carousels, and community posts all in one session.

Whether you're using Claude desktop or running Claude Code off your computer, I show you both paths so you can choose what works for your setup. And everything you need to get started is completely free in the HerMpire Builders community.

What's inside this video:

- How to install the Content Batch Day System skill in Claude desktop
- How to set up the skill inside Claude Code for faster, file-based batching
- How to build your content pack (brand voice, avatar, offers) so your AI output actually sounds like you
- How to use a blog post, YouTube transcript, or topic idea as your content source
- What types of content it produces across every platform

You don't need to set this up every time. You build the content pack once, and from that point on Claude knows who you are and what you create. That's how you go from spending hours on content every week to getting it all done in one sitting.

title: 💻 Claude Code Voice Mode and /batch: Coding Workflow
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code Voice Mode and /batch: Coding Workflow. Claude Code voice mode rolls out with push-to-talk and /voice command. Voice mode enables fully hands-free terminal coding: Claude Code voice mode introduces push-to-talk voice input directly in the terminal, allowing developers to describe code changes, request debugging, and navigate files without touching the keyboard. The /voice command activates the mode, and a configurable hotkey toggles the microphone. /simplify reduces code complexity with a single command: The /simplify command analyzes a function, module, or file and proposes targeted refactoring to reduce cognitive complexity, eliminate dead code, and improve readability. Anthropic began rolling out voice mode for Claude Code on March 3, 2026, giving approximately 5% of users the ability to control their terminal coding assistant entirely through spoken commands. The feature introduces a push-to-talk interface activated via the`/voice` command, alongside two new productivity commands: `/batch` for parallel task execution and `/simplify` for automated code refactoring. ## What Is Claude Code Voice Mode. Claude Code voice mode is a speech-to-text input layer for Anthropic's terminal-based coding assistant.
